payload.subject: Gate resolution: Sonnet re-score — all 3 conflicts resolved
payload.summary: User-authorized resolutions for P1, P2, P3. Gate should now open for execution.

resolutions.detail: PSQ uses its existing in-conversation labeling protocol. No API override. The no-API-scripts policy stands — the halo contamination revert demonstrates real risk. Use label_separated.py extract → score in Claude Code session → ingest. One dimension per session to maintain halo separation. ~9-10 sessions for full corpus.

Score ALL 10 dimensions with Sonnet (TE, HI, AD, ED, RC, RB, TC, CC, DA, CO). Do not drop CC or DA at scoring time. The psychology-agent will decide which dimensions to use at retrain time. This preserves the full dataset for future analysis and avoids invalidating criterion validity studies. adversarial_register remains outside PSQ's construct boundary — psychology-agent manages AR scoring independently. No construct revision needed.

P1 resolution toward in-conversation scoring dissolves this conflict. PSQ uses its own infrastructure. The 998 texts are the existing training corpus in psq.db — PSQ extracts them using its own tooling. No external scripts or data paths needed.

payload.authorization: User-approved. All three resolutions discussed and confirmed by the human principal investigator.

additional_context.b3_finding: Session 37 discovered isotonic calibration plateaus across all TE dimensions (not just HI). Largest TE plateau spans 1.51 raw units. Quantile-binned recalibration (n_bins=20) needed for all dims. This recalibration can happen independently of the Sonnet re-score — recommend running it before DistilBERT retrain so the improved calibration baseline propagates.
additional_context.downstream_chain: Sonnet re-score (this work order) → recalibrate all dims with quantile binning → DistilBERT retrain on Sonnet labels → factor analysis (PCA) on scored data

claims.text: All three conflict resolutions were discussed with and approved by the human principal investigator in Session 37.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct observation — AskUserQuestion tool responses in this session.

claims.text: TE isotonic calibration has 4 plateaus spanning >0.5 raw units. The largest covers 1.51 units (15% of scale). Root cause: sparsity in training data for those raw score regions.
claims.confidence_basis: Direct analysis of calibration.json x/y thresholds in Session 37.

epistemic_flags: C2 confidence 0.95: plateau analysis based on threshold inspection, not on actual sample counts per bin (training data not accessed)
